hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding by avoiding unfavorable steric clashes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 binding but lack sufficient selectivity. The next iteration should focus on optimizing non-pharmacophore positions to reduce off-target interactions while maintaining high-affinity binding to SSTR2.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current ddG threshold is too lenient, allowing candidates with weak binding (e.g., -20.2) to pass. Tightening the threshold will filter out weaker binders and focus on higher-affinity designs.
To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) that contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5).
